texts.full_text: Varvara Guljajeva , Mar Canet , and Sebastian Mealla Special thanx to Sytse WieRenga! We have plotted brainwave activity into a knitted pattern. Using a wearable, non-invasive EEG headset, we recorded users’ affective states while listening to Bach’s “Goldberg Variations”, concretely the aria and its first seven variations. The audio was about 10 minutes long and we downsampled each second of the signal coming from the 14 channels of the EEG device. Three main features were measured: relaxation, excitement, and cognitive load. Wampa "The kid ran into something, and it wasn't just the cold." texts.full_text: survey results, the customers were moderately satisfied with their experiences across the touch points, except for Communications from the Company and Future Product/Company Direction. Between 20% and 50% of the customers said they were dissatisfied with each of the seven customer touch points. Case Study: Relative Performance Assessment Results Results of the Relative Performance Assessment ratings are located in Figure 3. As you can see, customers said that only 42% of the customers indicated that the company was better than the competition. Almost 60% of the customers indicated that the company was the same worse than most other competitors. Figure 3. Relative Performance Assessment ratings of target company. After re-scaling the values of the 5-p0int rating scale (1 = worst to 5 = best) to a 0-100 scale, I estimated that the target company falls roughly at the 54th percentile in their industry; that is, the company’s performance is typical when compared to their competition. Case Study: Determining Dollar Value of Loyalty To estimate the expected revenue gains/losses of the target company, I worked with the investment firm to translate the customer loyalty ratings into a dollar value. We employed subject matter experts (SMEs) as well as analyzed existing financial reports of the target company to arrive at our best guess of expected annual revenue gains through new customers (~$300k)) and existing customers (through purchasing new/different products – ~$160k) and estimated the annual revenue at risk due to churn (by customers stop using the company ~ $450k). Case Study: The Decision Overall, the customer feedback confirmed the valuation of the company. While the target company was perceived to be in the middle of the pack

texts.full_text: drives a crossover to a ballistic regime in which the propagation along the wire is dominated by the chiral anomaly, and the role of fluctuations inside the sample is exponentially suppressed. We show that inelastic collisions modify the parametric dependence of the current fluctuations on the magnetic field. By measuring shot noise as a function of a magnetic field, for different applied voltage, one can estimate the electron-electron inelastic length $l_{\rm ee}$.' author: - 'P.G. Matveeva' - 'D.N. Aristov' - 'D. Meidan' - 'D.B. Gutman' title: Shot noise in Weyl semimetals --- Introduction ============ Weyl semimetals have been a subject of active experimental and theoretical research due to their unusual transport properties [@Turner; @Wan; @Burkov_2011; @Hosur; @Kim; @Xu; @Weng; @Huang; @Neupane; @Liu; @Xu_2015; @Weng_2015]. A Weyl semimetal is characterized by a three dimensional band structure where the conduction and valence bands touch at discrete points in momentum space, known as Weyl nodes. texts.full_text: I saw them. But it wasn’t like that with Amina and I wondered why? Why was everything different with her? Why had I allowed her to take up so much space in my world? And why am I so happy about it? “What’s up, Gabe?” I looked to see Russell Harper, the band’s saxophone player, sliding onto the stool beside me and that was when I realized that the band had finished and were taking a short break before their next set. “What’s up, Harp?” “Question is, what’s up with you?” “Nothing much.” Harp shook his head and said with a chuckle, “We both know that’s a lie.” I chose not to comment; mainly because it made no sense and two, I had Amina on my mind. Instead, I looked toward the door where a beautiful woman was waiting for Valerie to greet and then seat her. She was wearing an elegant black one-shoulder, sleeveless dress with an asymmetrical neckline that showed off her fit body. Bottom line, the woman was fine. texts.full_text: Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) School Loop complies fully with FERPA, which protects the privacy of students' educational records: - Parental Access: Parents or guardians can request access to review student data. - Consent Requirements: We do not disclose personally identifiable information from education records without written consent unless allowed by specific exceptions under FERPA. #### California Student Online Personal Information Protection Act (SOPIPA) School Loop is SOPIPA compliant, which means: - No Targeted Advertising: No personal student data or educational activities are used for targeted advertising. - Data Retention and Deletion Policies: We have clear policies regarding the retention of student information. Data that is no longer needed will be securely deleted. #### California Assembly Bill 1584 (AB1584) & Senate Bill 177 (SB177) School Loop adheres to these regulations, which: - Restrict Use and Sale: We do not sell student information or use it for any commercial purpose. - Security Requirements: Maintain robust security practices to protect against unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, or destruction of data. ### Data Encryption All sensitive data transmitted between users and the School Loop

One hypothesis suggests that excessive androgen production by theca interna cells may dysregulate the normal feedback loop between FSH and inhibin B, leading to an increase in FSH secretion ([@B7]). texts.full_text: tive feedback about Labs being loyal, intelligent, and great with children. They were known for having a playful yet patient nature that suited young families like theirs. Mrs. Smith also appreciated the breed's affectionate and social temperament. She imagined their future pup fitting seamlessly into their family dynamics, providing love and companionship to both the kids and herself when Mr. Smith was at work. The decision made, they began their search for a Labrador Retriever puppy. They found a listing online that caught their eye - a small local rescue organization had three black Lab puppies up for adoption. The children were thrilled about this discovery and insisted on going to meet them right away. Little did the Smiths know, their visit would lead them not just to one of these adorable pups but also to an unexpected journey into dog fostering and rehoming. As they approached the rescue, they were greeted warmly by a volunteer who guided them to where the puppies were being kept. There among the other dogs was a curious and energetic black Labrador pup wagging his tail eagerly - this one caught their attention instantly. He had big brown eyes and an even bigger personality that seemed to say, "Pick me!" The children squealed with excitement, wanting nothing more than for Mr. Smith to hold the lively little fellow right then and there. But before they could act on impulse, Mrs. Smith suggested they speak to someone from the rescue about what bringing a puppy into their home would entail. They were introduced to Maura Furie, who explained that adopting a dog isn’t just about finding one you like; it's also understanding how to make them feel comfortable and secure in your new family setting. This concept resonated deeply with the Smiths as they recognized that their puppy would come from an uncertain past which needed nurturing love more

texts.full_text: f the active layer low. A thinner film between the electrodes can lower the probability for charge recombination which lowers device efficiency, and can also increase the carrier drift velocity due to higher electric field. However, the optical absorption will be low in such thin films. Thus, there is a conflict between the optical length scale and the electronic length scale. Another problem is charge recombination. Electron-hole pairs generated within the donor polymer on absorption of light still recombine if they do not find an acceptor interface within their lifetime. These electron-hole pairs are called singlet excitons. Even if these singlet excitons dissociate, the electron on the acceptor and the hole on the donor form charge-transfer excitons, which can again recombine. texts.full_text: may also stop playing as much. Younger children are drawn to snow days. As a child, snow days are something to be looked forward to with glee. As we become adults, they are dreaded because they interfere with work, with appointments, with our well-planned lives. Still, there is usually a part of us that retains the child's perspective, no matter how "mature" we seem to be. For elders, the snow day may hold less power, perhaps providing more entertainment than enjoyment. The entertainment comes from all the younger people working so hard to tame nature, to get things done, to behave as if nature did not have the power to change our lives. For the elders, we already know the inexorable power of nature, in the form of the passage of time. At their best, snow days give us pause to reflect upon our busy lives. These are times when we can take advantage of the art of non-doing. For a day or two, we may not need to do anything in order to accomplish all that needs to be done. To enter this taoist realm is a gift, but a bewildering gift to many of us. We struggle to do things, to continue our habits and patterns, to do that which is expected of us. Some of us eventually let go of those feelings and enter the world of non-doing. Most of us don't. texts.full_text: EDFA typically includes an erbium-doped fiber (EDF) segment 16, which is optically pumped by one or more pump lasers 18 via a wavelength-division multiplexer (WDM) coupler 20. The pump light, usually at wavelengths of 980 nm or 1480 nm, excites erbium ions in the doped fiber core to a higher energy state. When the signal light (typically in the 1550 nm band) propagates through the EDF, it stimulates the excited erbium ions to emit photons coherently, thereby amplifying the signal. The amplified signal is then coupled into the output transmission fiber 14 via another WDM coupler 22, which separates the residual pump light from the amplified signal. Conventional EDFAs offer several advantages, including high gain (often exceeding 20 dB), low noise figure, and compatibility with wavelength-division multiplexing (WDM) systems. These characteristics make EDFAs indispensable in long-haul communication networks, where they compensate for signal attenuation over distances exceeding 100 km. However, traditional EDFA designs face challenges in applications requiring ultra-narrow linewidths, tunable output, or integration with other photonic components, particularly in emerging fiber laser architectures. Fiber lasers, which utilize doped fiber as both the gain medium and waveguide, have gained prominence for their scalability, beam quality, and efficiency. In such systems, the EDFA's amplification mechanism is often adapted to generate coherent laser output by incorporating optical feedback elements (e.g., fiber Bragg gratings) to form a resonant cavity. texts.full_text: the collection. Also the output is a bit confusing as no...ConcurrentModificationException is thrown. This behavior might seem counterintuitive at first, but it's actually due to how ConcurrentHashMap works internally. When you call `map.keySet().iterator()`, you get an iterator that views the current state of the map. However, because this is a fail-safe iterator on a ConcurrentHashMap, which is designed for concurrent access and modifications, the iteration itself does not block or prevent other threads (or even the same thread) from modifying the map. The key point here is understanding what "fail-safe" means in this context: it doesn't mean that the original collection remains untouched during iteration. Instead, it ensures that you won't get a ConcurrentModificationException when iterating over the collection while it's being modified. This is because the iterator works on a snapshot of the keys at the time the iterator was created (in terms of visibility to this thread) for most operations. However, your code snippet shows updates happening directly on the map (`map.put("c", "three");`, etc.), and these changes are indeed reflected in the original map. This might seem contradictory because we mentioned that fail-safe iterators work with a snapshot or view of the data at iteration start time, but this primarily concerns how they handle structural modifications (like adding/removing elements) during iteration. In your case, when you call `map.put("c", "three");` inside the loop, it's not the iterator doing these updates; it's the direct method calls on the map. These operations update the original map because that's what these methods are designed to do - modify the map directly. The fail-safe aspect of the iterator doesn't apply here since you're explicitly modifying the collection outside of Its iteration logic. So, after your loop finishes and when you print `map.get("q")`, it correctly shows "four" because those keys were indeed added to the original map during the execution of the loop. In summary: - The fail-safe iterator ensures that iterating over a view (like keySet()) won't throw ConcurrentModificationException if the underlying collection changes. -

texts.full_text: was frustrated that papers were not self-contained. As a result I tried to make each paper fully understandable to an outsider. This won me some readers in the general public but (likely) cost me several early conference rejections. Longwinded front-matter in conference papers (less applicable to journal) is bad for the following reasons: (1) Reviewers read 5-10 papers per conference and 50-100 papers per year in very similar areas. The basics will bore them. (2) If your contribution starts on page 5/8, you have very little excuse for having failed to do anything the reviewer asks for. There are two issues at stake here: knowing your audience and positioning intelligently. Most of your abstract (by sentences), your intro (by paragraphs), and your paper (by pages) should articulate what you do. Anticipate the reader’s questions and answer them in the paper A good reviewer will try to come up with critical questions to challenge the proposed work. Is it possible that this method only works because X? If the answer is “I don’t know” and “no” would be damning, your paper might rightly be rejected. If you can anticipate the question and know the answer, write it. If you do not know the answer, then run an experiment to find out. I hope this point hits home that doing strong research and writing clearly are tightly linked. Style The Scientific “We” In scientific writing, narrate with the pronoun “we”. This style serves a didactic purpose: “we” refers to “you” (the reader) and “I/we” (the authors) together. Sometimes, you may need to express an opinion. These cases should be made clear from context. Avoid hostages to fortune Any qualified reader, who goes through your entire draft, even if they do not share your opinions, preference for methods, or values in life, should be unable to disagree with any sentence in isolation. “Our method X outperforms Y on most datasets.” Does it? Most out of what collection of datasets? Could your reviewer choose some dataset repository and find the statement false? Better to say “many” datasets. This is both bett

I've manually emptied cache folders and have tried changing PHP versions. Any other steps I can take to figure out what's going on? A: In my experience a white screen is always a suppressed PHP error. If you can't get the error to show, something is still suppressing it. Instead of going back and forth with comments, we jumped on Skype. In this case, disabling extensions in the config.php file got the PHP error to show up on the screen. This is an EE1 site so we used: $conf['allow_extensions'] = "n"; It turns out a It turns out a specific extension was causing the issue. By disabling all extensions and then selectively re-enabling them, you can identify which one is causing the problem. Once identified, you can either update the problematic extension to a compatible version or look for an alternative solution that doesn't conflict with your setup. Here are some additional steps you could take: 1. Check PHP Error Reporting: Ensure that PHP error reporting is set to display errors. Add the following lines at the top of your `index.php` file in the ExpressionEngine root directory: ```php ini_set('display_errors', 1); ini_set('display_startup_errors', 1); error_reporting(E_ALL); ``` 2. Review Extension Compatibility: Make sure all installed extensions are compatible with EE 1.x. Some extensions may not be supported or may have issues with older versions of ExpressionEngine. 3. Check PHP Version: Ensure that the PHP version you are using is compatible with EE 1.x. Sometimes, newer PHP versions can cause compatibility issues with older software. 4. texts.full_text: be a Freddie Mercury fan if he knew Freddie’s life story? Freddie Mercury By Guest Contributor: Lakshmi Gandhi (@LakshmiGandhi) Before journalist Jarrett Hill broke the story of Melania Trump’s alleged plagiarism of Michelle Obama’s famous 2008 convention speech, it looked like one of the biggest social media moments of the night was the shock and dismay of music fans everywhere as Donald Trump entered the convention hall to Queen's "We are The Champions." Twitter promptly exploded with a mixture of memes highlighting how no one saw this coming, along with GIFs set to Queen’s other hits like “Bohemian Rhapsody” and “Don’t Stop Me Now.” As it turns out though, Trump's appreciation for the legendary rock band may have been deeper than many realized; in interviews over the years, he has expressed his admiration not only for their music but also for Freddie Mercury as a person—despite knowing about the singer's life story which included being openly gay and living with HIV/AIDS before passing away from complications related to it. It raises an interesting question: Would Donald Trump still be a fan of Freddie Mercury if he knew everything about his personal journey? While we can never truly know someone else’s motivations or feelings, there are some insights that might help us understand why Queen and their lead singer could have such appeal across different demographics—including conservative figures like Trump. One way to reduce the amount of computation required in a environment is to eliminate non-useful peaks. Existing noise removing algorithms are limited in their data-reduction capability and are compute intensive making them unsuitable for big data and high-throughput environments. In this paper we introduce a novel low-complexity technique based on classification, quantization and sampling of MS peaks. We present a novel data-reductive strategy for analysis of Big MS data. Our algorithm, called MS-REDUCE, is capable of eliminating noisy peaks as well as peaks that do not contribute to peptide deduction before any peptide deduction is attempted. texts.full_text: ults Full access to all collections is a privilege of BUFBrian Hayes: A Scholarly Exploration into the Life and Work of a Pioneering British Filmmaker Brian Hayes, a name that resonates with the golden age of British cinema, was a visionary filmmaker whose work has left an indelible mark on the landscape of British moving image and sound. As we delve into the vast archives of the British Universities Film & Video Council (BUFVC), we uncover a wealth of information that illuminates the life and legacy of this remarkable individual. texts.full_text: ie, medical versus surgical in the pediatric emergency department (PED). A retrospective chart review of a convenience sample of consecutive patients, excluding those seen during times when both pediatric and emergency medicine residents were not simultaneously present. Urban Municipal PED with 25,000 visits annually. Pediatric residents, emergency medicine residents, and medical students rotating through the PED and their supervising attending physicians. None. Five hundred and ninety-nine charts were included in the study. On the basis of the triage complaint, patients were categorized as medical or surgical. The primary outcome was the proportion of patients selected for further evaluation by each group of residents. Secondary outcomes included the proportion of patients admitted to the hospital, the proportion of patients who underwent imaging studies, and the proportion of patients who had laboratory tests performed. Results showed that pediatric residents selected a significantly higher proportion of medical patients compared to emergency medicine residents (85.5% vs 73.4%, p=0.01). Similarly, emergency medicine residents selected a higher proportion of surgical patients compared to pediatric residents (86.7% vs 79.3%, p=0.04). There was no significant difference in the proportion of patients admitted to the hospital between the two groups (pediatric residents: 18.5%, emergency medicine residents: 20.9%, p=0.38). However, pediatric residents performed a higher proportion of imaging studies (pediatric residents: 42.2%, emergency medicine residents: 33.1%, p=0.03) and laboratory tests (pediatric residents: 68.4%, emergency medicine residents: 56.6%, p=0.01). Conclusion: Pediatric and emergency medicine residents exhibit bias when selecting patients from triage based on the chief complaint in the pediatric emergency department. This bias may impact the types of patients evaluated and the resources utilized in the emergency department. Further research is needed to explore the implications of this bias on
